prompt: Create a square mixed-media urban poster portrait of an adult man shown from mid-torso up, centered and facing forward, with the face deliberately obscured by one solid opaque square censor block in warm brown covering the eyes, nose, and mouth. The subject has short dark hair visible above the block and a short beard visible below it, wearing a layered streetwear outfit: a white T-shirt, thin silver chain necklace with a small vertical pendant, a black hooded sweatshirt, and a glossy blue-black-white-orange puffer jacket with sharp collage-like panels. The background is a distressed paper wall collage with torn newspaper fragments, faded handwritten notes, black ink scribbles, and heavy abstract brush strokes in cobalt blue, red-orange, off-white, black, and beige. Include legible poster-style fragments: “VOIR LES FLEURS SAU-VAGES” on the upper left, “breathe repeat” and partial red block lettering near the upper right, and “ne pas telement” in small type on the left. Use gritty high-contrast textures, screen-print halftone, paint splatter, scratches, tape-like edges, and torn-paper overlays across both the clothing and background, blending photography with punk zine collage and expressive street art. Keep the composition tightly cropped, square 4:5-feeling poster framing, dramatic detail in the jacket and textures, no clean studio background, no extra people, no watermark.
